message:
perf(tui): dual-cadence tick system and tool/test updates

Replace the fixed 100ms (10fps) TUI tick with a dual-cadence system:
FAST_TICK_INTERVAL (100ms) during active animations, pending toasts,
recordings, or remote terminal activity; IDLE_TICK_INTERVAL (500ms/2fps)
when quiescent. The MailAppModel tracks `scheduled_tick_interval` and
only emits Cmd::tick when the cadence actually changes, via the new
`desired_tick_interval()` and `update_tick_schedule()` methods.

Add `prefers_fast_tick()` to the Screen trait (default false) with a
dashboard implementation that signals when fast ticks are needed.

Also extends mcp-agent-mail-tools with additional tool functionality
and updates a system_error_parity test assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2314,7 +2314,7 @@ fn run_tui_main_thread(
         .with_mouse()
         .with_diff_config(stable_tui_diff_config())
         .with_budget(ftui_render::budget::FrameBudgetConfig {
-            total: Duration::from_millis(100), // Match TICK_INTERVAL
+            total: Duration::from_millis(100), // Match FAST_TICK_INTERVAL
             allow_frame_skip: false,           // Never blank frames
             degradation_cooldown: 5,           // 500ms between level changes
             upgrade_threshold: 0.5,
